findings.evidence: roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/UniversalCovers/README.md:64-74 ('credit the original authors and source PR in each file'; #31576 'For X semilocally simply connected'; #38292 'under [... SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]'). grep for 'SemilocallySimplyConnected' in ./mathlib returns no topology hit, so the hypothesis used by those PRs is not in Mathlib master.
findings.fix: If the predicate/API follows #31576 or #38292, cite the PR and its authors in the file header; if it is an independent restatement of the standard (e.g. Hatcher) notion, say so explicitly and reconcile with the roadmap's port-and-credit directive.
findings.issue: The References section credits only the roadmap generically. The roadmap identifies #31576 and #38292 as the human-authored source PRs for the universal-cover construction, both stated under the `SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace` hypothesis (Mathlib master has no such predicate), and directs crediting the original authors and source PR in each ported file. The PR description instead claims the predicate is 'genuinely new.'

summary: The predicate is the standing hypothesis the roadmap ties to specific human-authored unmerged Mathlib PRs (#31576/#38292), which are stated over `[SemilocallySimplyConnectedSpace X]` and which the roadmap directs be credited per-file; the file cites only the roadmap generically and claims the notion is new. If the predicate follows those PRs it must credit them.
